findings.title: Configuration is valid
findings.observed: fixture topology includes redundant sources, followers, proxyd, and interop dependency intent
findings.recommendation: Use this as a deterministic example report; live validation still requires opstack-doctor check against operator-owned endpoints.

findings.title: Execution candidate is op-reth/reth
findings.recommendation: Continue running the candidate beside the reference and comparing hashes, state roots, and RPC outputs before migration cutover.

findings.title: Execution candidate head is close to reference
findings.recommendation: Keep monitoring candidate lag until cutover; this fixture represents a healthy steady state.

findings.title: Execution blocks match
findings.observed: compared 16 common blocks with matching hashes and roots
findings.recommendation: Keep this comparison in pre-cutover automation so op-reth divergence is caught before serving traffic.

findings.title: Execution RPC samples match
findings.observed: sampled read-only RPC outputs matched between reference and candidate
findings.recommendation: Expand sampled methods cautiously as operator confidence and endpoint compatibility requirements grow.

findings.title: op-node reports up
findings.recommendation: Keep source node availability as a hard dependency in dashboards and alerting.

findings.title: op-node peer count is above threshold
findings.recommendation: Keep P2P peer count alerting enabled for source nodes.

findings.title: Follower RPC head tracks source
findings.recommendation: Use source nodes as the dependency tier for light and sequencer op-nodes; keep follower lag alerting in place.

findings.title: Follower safe head tracks source
findings.recommendation: Treat source safe-head freshness as a dependency for follower health.

findings.title: Deriver proxyd is consensus-aware
findings.observed: consensus-aware routing intent declared with 2 source backends
findings.recommendation: Keep consensus-aware proxyd in front of production consumers that need protection from divergent backends.

findings.title: Interop fixture scope
findings.observed: basic dependency readiness only; no cross-chain message validation is implied
findings.recommendation: Use this fixture to understand output shape. Live interop readiness still requires following every dependency chain and protocol-specific validation.

findings.title: Interop dependency RPC is reachable
findings.recommendation: Keep dependency-set RPC and metrics endpoints observable before enabling deeper interop validation.
